title: Cyphomandra luteoalba
taxonomy: Cyphomandra luteoalba is classified as a species within the genus Cyphomandra and family Solanaceae. The genus Cyphomandra is part of the order Solanales. It is native to the Americas. The family Solanaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many economically important species. Some members of this family are also known as the nightshade family.
uses: Cyphomandra luteoalba has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices. Its extracts have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have wound-healing properties, as well as potential uses in treating various skin conditions and infections. Furthermore, some studies suggest that Cyphomandra luteoalba may have antiviral and anticancer properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

side_effects: Cyphomandra luteoalba is known to have various side effects due to its toxicity. The plant contains solanine, a glycoalkaloid that can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in excessive consumption. It may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and stomach pain in some individuals. Additionally, long-term exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause more severe health issues, including organ damage and neurological problems. intro: Cyphomandra luteoalba, also known as the yellow pitaya or golden dragon fruit, is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Cyphomandra luteoalba include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, capsules, and infusions, which are used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions.
